blocks.original_text: Tyrion Lannister was starved, but he refused to let this brute see him cringe. “A leg of lamb would be pleasant,” he said, from the heap of soiled straw in the corner of his cell. “Perhaps a dish of peas and onions, some fresh baked bread with butter, and a flagon of mulled wine to wash it down. Or beer, if that’s easier. I try not to be overly particular.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion饥饿却不肯示弱，故意点出一整套丰盛餐食。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 精致菜单不是认真点餐，而是以语言保住身份感；身体被控制时，他先控制谈话的风格。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion sighed. The turnkey was twenty stone of gross stupidity, with brown rotting teeth and small dark eyes. The left side of his face was slick with scar where an axe had cut off his ear and part of his cheek. He was as predictable as he was ugly, but Tyrion was hungry. He reached up for the plate.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion评估Mord粗暴、愚钝而可预测，仍因饥饿伸手。

blocks.original_text: Mord jerked it away, grinning. “Is here,” he said, holding it out beyond Tyrion’s reach.

blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion忍痛站起，厌恶每餐都重复这种羞辱游戏。

blocks.original_text: Mord shambled backward, grinning through his rotten teeth. “Is here, dwarf man.” He held the plate out at arm’s length, over the edge where the cell ended and the sky began. “You not want eat? Here. Come take.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord退到sky cell边缘诱他靠近取食。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 食物被放到edge外，把最基本需求变成服从与冒险测试；Mord的娱乐建立在身体差异上。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion’s arms were too short to reach the plate, and he was not about to step that close to the edge. All it would take would be a quick shove of Mord’s heavy white belly, and he would end up a sickening red splotch on the stones of Sky, like so many other prisoners of the Eyrie over the centuries. “Come to think on it, I’m not hungry after all,” he declared, retreating to the corner of his cell.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion怕被推下去，宁愿宣称不饿也不冒险。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion拒绝靠近不是怯懦，而是正确识别对方可用一个shove制造‘escape’假象。

blocks.original_text: Mord grunted and opened his thick fingers. The wind took the plate, flipping it over as it fell. A handful of beans sprayed back at them as the food tumbled out of sight. The turnkey laughed, his gut shaking like a bowl of pudding.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord把beans扔下悬崖并大笑。

blocks.original_text: For that, Mord gave him a kick, driving a steel-toed boot hard into Tyrion’s ribs on the way out. “I take it back!” he gasped as he doubled over on the straw. “I’ll kill you myself, I swear it!” The heavy iron-bound door slammed shut. Tyrion heard the rattle of keys.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord踢伤他离开；Tyrion痛中仍威胁亲手杀他。

blocks.original_text: For a small man, he had been cursed with a dangerously big mouth, he reflected as he crawled back to his corner of what the Arryns laughably called their dungeon. He huddled beneath the thin blanket that was his only bedding, staring out at a blaze of empty blue sky and distant mountains that seemed to go on forever, wishing he still had the shadowskin cloak he’d won from Marillion at dice, after the singer had stolen it off the body of that brigand chief. The skin had smelled of blood and mold, but it was warm and thick. Mord had taken it the moment he laid eyes on it.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion反省自己的大嘴，缩在薄毯下想念被Mord夺走的shadowskin cloak。
blocks.literary_note_zh: big mouth既是自我批评也是全章结构线索：同一张嘴先致祸，后成为唯一逃生工具。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: The wind tugged at his blanket with gusts sharp as talons. His cell was miserably small, even for a dwarf. Not five feet away, where a wall ought to have been, where a wall would be in a proper dungeon, the floor ended and the sky began. He had plenty of fresh air and sunshine, and the moon and stars by night, but Tyrion would have traded it all in an instant for the dankest, gloomiest pit in the bowels of the Casterly Rock.
blocks.explanation_zh: sky cell没有外墙，风寒如爪；他宁愿要Casterly Rock最阴湿的地牢。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mord以食物和暴力折磨Tyrion；sky cell的风、斜坡与高度持续侵蚀身体和睡眠，他反复后悔自己的嘴。
blocks.background_zh: **sky cell：** Eyrie临悬崖的开放牢房，无外墙且地板向外倾斜，以寒风、失眠和坠落恐惧逼迫囚犯。

blocks.original_text: “You fly,” Mord had promised him, when he’d shoved him into the cell. “Twenty day, thirty, fifty maybe. Then you fly.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord曾预言他被关若干天后会飞下去。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mord以食物和暴力折磨Tyrion；sky cell的风、斜坡与高度持续侵蚀身体和睡眠，他反复后悔自己的嘴。

blocks.original_text: The Arryns kept the only dungeon in the realm where the prisoners were welcome to escape at will. That first day, after girding up his courage for hours, Tyrion had lain flat on his stomach and squirmed to the edge, to poke out his head and look down. Sky was six hundred feet below, with nothing between but empty air. If he craned his neck out as far as it could go, he could see other cells to his right and left and above him. He was a bee in a stone honeycomb, and someone had torn off his wings.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion曾趴到边缘确认六百英尺落差，把自己比作被拔翼的蜂。
blocks.literary_note_zh: bee in a stone honeycomb却无wings，把Eyrie建筑秩序写成专为坠落而设计的自然陷阱。
blocks.background_zh: **Sky：** Eyrie下方的中继城堡，距sky cells约六百英尺；并非抽象天空名称。

blocks.original_text: It was cold in the cell, the wind screamed night and day, and worst of all, the floor sloped. Ever so slightly, yet it was enough. He was afraid to close his eyes, afraid that he might roll over in his steep and wake in sudden terror as he went sliding off the edge. Small wonder the sky cells drove men mad.
blocks.explanation_zh: 倾斜地面令他不敢睡，解释sky cells为何使人发疯。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 倾斜几乎不可见，却持续剥夺睡眠；sky cell通过环境而非正式刑讯击溃人。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Gods save me, some previous tenant had written on the wall in something that looked suspiciously like blood, the blue is calling. At first Tyrion wondered who he’d been, and what had become of him; later, he decided that he would rather not know.
blocks.explanation_zh: 墙上疑似血写的旧留言说blue正在召唤；Tyrion不愿知道前囚犯结局。
blocks.literary_note_zh: the blue is calling把天空由自由象征改写为自杀诱惑，颜色本身获得主动声音。

blocks.original_text: The wretched boy had started it, looking down on him from a throne of carved weirwood beneath the moon-and-falcon banners of House Arryn. Tyrion Lannister had been looked down on all his life, but seldom by rheumy-eyed six-year-olds who needed to stuff fat cushions under their cheeks to lift them to the height of a man. “Is he the bad man?” the boy had asked, clutching his doll.
blocks.explanation_zh: 回忆中，六岁的Robert在Eyrie王座上问Tyrion是否坏人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: looked down on同时是空间动作与Tyrion一生承受的社会姿态，双关把Robert的高座变成旧伤。
blocks.background_zh: **Robert Arryn：** Jon Arryn与Lysa的六岁儿子、Lord of the Eyrie；身体与情绪状态脆弱，由Lysa强力保护。

blocks.original_text: “He is,” the Lady Lysa had said from the lesser throne beside him. She was all in blue, powdered and perfumed for the suitors who filled her court.
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa当众回答是。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 回忆抵达Eyrie后的公开冲突：Tyrion因羞耻与怒气不断讥讽Lysa母子，终于被送入sky cell。

blocks.original_text: “This is Tyrion the Imp, of House Lannister, who murdered your father.” She raised her voice so it carried down the length of High Hall of the Eyrie, ringing off the milk-white walls and the slender pillars, so every man could hear it. “He slew the Hand of the King!”

blocks.original_text: “Oh, did I kill him too?” Tyrion had said, like a fool.

blocks.original_text: That would have been a very good time to have kept his mouth closed and his head bowed. He could see that now; seven hells, he had seen it then. The High Hall of the Arryns was long and austere, with a forbidding coldness to its walls of blue-veined white marble, but the faces around him had been colder by far. The power of Casterly Rock was far away, and there were no friends of the Lannisters in the Vale of Arryn. Submission and silence would have been his best defenses.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他明知沉默屈服才是最佳防御，却面对Vale全无友军。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他清楚最佳策略却做不到，说明聪明不等于能在羞耻与愤怒中执行最佳判断。

blocks.original_text: But Tyrion’s mood had been too foul for sense. To his shame, he had faltered during the last leg of their day-long climb up to the Eyrie, his stunted legs unable to take him any higher. Bronn had carried him the rest of the way, and the humiliation poured oil on the flames of his anger. “It would seem I’ve been a busy little fellow,” he said with bitter sarcasm. “I wonder when I found the time to do all this slaying and murdering.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 登山时被Bronn背负的羞耻加剧怒火，使他继续讽刺自己过分忙于杀人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bronn背他本是帮助，Tyrion却因长期被羞辱而体验为身份受损，怒气遂误投向庭审。

blocks.original_text: “Imp,” Lysa said coldly, “you will guard that mocking tongue of yours and speak to my son politely, or I promise you will have cause to regret it. Remember where you are. This is the Eyrie, and these are knights of the Vale you see around you, true men who loved Jon Arryn well. Every one of them would die for me.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa警告他礼貌说话，并以Vale knights的忠诚压制他。

blocks.original_text: “The Eyrie is impregnable,” Lysa Arryn declared calmly. She drew her son close, holding him safe in the circle of her plump white arms. “The Imp is trying to frighten us, sweet baby. The Lannisters are all liars. No one will hurt my sweet boy.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa拥住Robert，宣称Eyrie不可攻破、Lannisters全是liars。

blocks.original_text: The hell of it was, she was no doubt right. Having seen what it took to get here, Tyrion could well imagine how it would be for a knight trying to fight his way up in armor, while stones and arrows poured down from above and enemies contested with him for every step. Nightmare did not begin to describe it. Small wonder the Eyrie had never been taken.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion根据艰难山路承认Eyrie确实几乎无法强攻。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 回忆抵达Eyrie后的公开冲突：Tyrion因羞耻与怒气不断讥讽Lysa母子，终于被送入sky cell。
blocks.background_zh: **Eyrie防御：** 城堡位于高山顶，通路狭窄陡峭；进攻者须沿多重山门攀升，因此历史上从未被攻陷。

blocks.original_text: Still, Tyrion had been unable to silence himself. “Not impregnable,” he said, “merely inconvenient.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 他仍忍不住说这里只是inconvenient，不是impregnable。
blocks.literary_note_zh: merely inconvenient是机智也是失控：逻辑上细微修正，政治上却再次刺伤主人自尊。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Young Robert pointed down, his hand trembling. “You’re a liar. Mother, I want to see him fly.” Two guardsmen in sky-blue cloaks seized Tyrion by the arms, lifting him off his floor.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert要求看他fly，guards抓住Tyrion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 回忆抵达Eyrie后的公开冲突：Tyrion因羞耻与怒气不断讥讽Lysa母子，终于被送入sky cell。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: The gods only know what might have happened then were it not for Catelyn Stark. “Sister,” she called out from where she stood below the thrones, “I beg you to remember, this man is my prisoner. I will not have him harmed.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn及时主张Tyrion是自己的prisoner，不准Lysa伤害。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以prisoner ownership阻止即时伤害，而不是在此宣布Tyrion无罪。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Lysa Arryn glanced at her sister coolly for a moment, then rose and swept down on Tyrion, her long skirts trailing after her. For an instant he feared she would strike him, but instead she commanded them to release him. Her men shoved him to the floor, his legs went out from under him, and Tyrion fell.
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa让guards放开，却任他们把Tyrion摔倒。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 回忆抵达Eyrie后的公开冲突：Tyrion因羞耻与怒气不断讥讽Lysa母子，终于被送入sky cell。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: He must have made quite a sight as he struggled to his knees, only to feel his right leg spasm, sending him sprawling once more. Laughter boomed up and down the High Hall of the Arryns.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion腿抽搐再次倒地，满堂哄笑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 大厅笑声把身体疼痛变成公共娱乐，解释Tyrion为何更依赖讽刺反夺观众控制。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “My sister’s little guest is too weary to stand,” Lady Lysa announced. “Ser Vardis, take him down to the dungeon. A rest in one of our sky cells will do him much good.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa以休息为名命Ser Vardis把他送入sky cell。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 回忆抵达Eyrie后的公开冲突：Tyrion因羞耻与怒气不断讥讽Lysa母子，终于被送入sky cell。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: The guardsmen jerked him upright. Tyrion Lannister dangled between them, kicking feebly, his face red with shame. “I will remember this,” he told them all as they carried him off.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion被架走时羞怒地说会记住这一切。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 回忆抵达Eyrie后的公开冲突：Tyrion因羞耻与怒气不断讥讽Lysa母子，终于被送入sky cell。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: And so he did, for all the good it did him.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他确实记得，却暂时毫无用处。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 短句用冷幽默切回现实：记忆能保存债务，却不能自动转化为权力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: At first he had consoled himself that this imprisonment could not last long. Lysa Arryn wanted to humble him, that was all. She would send for him again, and soon. If not her, then Catelyn Stark would want to question him. This time he would guard his tongue more closely. They dare not kill him out of hand; he was still a Lannister of Casterly Rock, and if they shed his blood, it would mean war. Or so he had told himself.
blocks.explanation_zh: 起初Tyrion相信关押只是羞辱，且Lannister身份和war风险能保命。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion推演外界反应并比较两桩案件的手法，怀疑有人利用Stark–Lannister冲突，决定靠谈话逃生。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Now he was not so certain.
blocks.explanation_zh: 现在他不再确定。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion推演外界反应并比较两桩案件的手法，怀疑有人利用Stark–Lannister冲突，决定靠谈话逃生。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Perhaps his captors only meant to let him rot here, but he feared he did not have the strength to rot for long. He was growing weaker every day, and it was only a matter of time until Mord’s kicks and blows did him serious harm, provided the gaoler did not starve him to death first. A few more nights of cold and hunger, and the blue would start calling to him too.
blocks.explanation_zh: 饥饿、寒冷和Mord殴打正在削弱他，blue也可能开始召唤。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion推演外界反应并比较两桩案件的手法，怀疑有人利用Stark–Lannister冲突，决定靠谈话逃生。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: He wondered what was happening beyond the walls (such as they were) of his cell. Lord Tywin would surely have sent out riders when the word reached him. Jaime might be leading a host through the Mountains of the Moon even now . . . unless he was riding north against Winterfell instead. Did anyone outside the Vale even suspect where Catelyn Stark had taken him? He wondered what Cersei would do when she heard. The king could order him freed, but would Robert listen to his queen or his Hand? Tyrion had no illusions about the king’s love for his sister.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他推演Tywin、Jaime、Cersei、Robert与Eddard可能采取的行动，却不知道外界是否知其位置。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 一连串问句展示他以政治模拟抵抗信息隔绝；每条推演都受‘无人知位置’这个关键不确定性限制。
blocks.background_zh: **可能反应：** Tywin、Jaime、Cersei、Robert和Eddard各有政治能力，但Tyrion不知道消息是否已传出Vale。

blocks.original_text: If Cersei kept her wits about her, she would insist the king sit in judgment of Tyrion himself. Even Ned Stark could scarcely object to that, not without impugning the honor of the king. And Tyrion would be only too glad to take his chances in a trial. Whatever murders they might lay at his door, the Starks had no proof of anything so far as he could see. Let them make their case before the Iron Throne and the lords of the land. It would be the end of them. If only Cersei were clever enough to see that . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: 他认为Cersei若冷静应要求king亲审；Starks缺乏证据，公开trial对自己有利。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion推演外界反应并比较两桩案件的手法，怀疑有人利用Stark–Lannister冲突，决定靠谈话逃生。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion Lannister sighed. His sister was not without a certain low cunning, but her pride blinded her. She would see the insult in this, not the opportunity. And Jaime was even worse, rash and headstrong and quick to anger. His brother never untied a knot when he could slash it in two with his sword.
blocks.explanation_zh: 但他判断Cersei受pride蒙蔽，Jaime更习惯用剑斩结而非解结。
blocks.literary_note_zh: untied a knot / slash it in two是Tyrion对Jaime决策风格的凝练比喻：即时行动可能破坏更大结构。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: He wondered which of them had sent the footpad to silence the Stark boy, and whether they had truly conspired at the death of Lord Arryn. If the old Hand had been murdered, it was deftly and subtly done. Men of his age died of sudden illness all the time. In contrast, sending some oaf with a stolen knife after Brandon Stark struck him as unbelievably clumsy. And wasn’t that peculiar, come to think on it . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion思考是谁派footpad刺杀Bran，并把精巧的Jon Arryn之死与笨拙袭童案对比。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他首次把两案的作案风格作比较；clumsy与subtle不匹配，是怀疑共同凶手叙事的重要逻辑裂缝。
blocks.background_zh: **文本事实／推断：** Tyrion承认不知道两案真相；他只从作案手法差异推断可能有别的操盘者。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion shivered. Now there was a nasty suspicion. Perhaps the direwolf and the lion were not the only beasts in the woods, and if that was true, someone was using him as a catspaw. Tyrion Lannister hated being used.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他怀疑还有第三方在利用direwolf与lion冲突，把自己当catspaw。
blocks.literary_note_zh: catspaw既指被利用的人，也回扣袭击Bran的凶器与执行者；Tyrion怀疑自己被安排成替罪对象。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: He would have to get out of here, and soon. His chances of overpowering Mord were small to none, and no one was about to smuggle him a six-hundred-foot-long rope, so he would have to talk himself free. His mouth had gotten him into this cell; it could damn well get him out.
blocks.explanation_zh: 因此必须尽快逃出；既无力制服Mord也无绳可逃，只能靠嘴。
blocks.literary_note_zh: mouth从缺陷改为resource，章法由自责转向主动设计。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion pushed himself to his feet, doing his best to ignore the slope of the floor beneath him, with its ever-so-subtle tug toward the edge. He hammered on the door with a fist. “Mord!” he shouted. “Turnkey! Mord, I want you!” He had to keep it up a good ten minutes before he heard footsteps. Tyrion stepped back an instant before the door opened with a crash.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion忍住斜坡恐惧，持续敲门召来Mord。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “Making noise,” Mord growled, with blood in his eyes. Dangling from one meaty hand was a leather strap, wide and thick, doubled over in his fist.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord带着皮带怒气冲冲出现。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Never show them you’re afraid, Tyrion reminded himself. “How would you like to be rich?” he asked.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion压住恐惧，直接问Mord想不想发财。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Never show them fear不是无恐惧，而是在恐惧存在时选择Mord能理解的欲望入口。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Mord hit him. He swung the strap backhand, lazily, but the leather caught Tyrion high on the arm. The force of it staggered him, and the pain made him grit his teeth. “No mouth, dwarf man,” Mord warned him.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord用皮带打他并命他闭嘴。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “Gold,” Tyrion said, miming a smile. “Casterly Rock is full of gold . . . ahhhh . . . ” This time the blow was a forehand, and Mord put more of his arm into the swing, making the leather crack and jump. It caught Tyrion in the ribs and dropped him to his knees, wimpering. He forced himself to look up at the gaoler. “As rich as the Lannisters,” he wheezed. “That’s what they say, Mord—”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion边挨打边重复Casterly Rock的gold诱惑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Mord grunted. The strap whistled through the air and smashed Tyrion full in the face. The pain was so bad he did not remember falling, but when he opened his eyes again he was on the floor of his cell. His ear was ringing, and his mouth was full of blood. He groped for purchase, to push himself up, and his fingers brushed against . . . nothing. Tyrion snatched his hand back as fast as if it had been scalded, and tried his best to stop breathing. He had fallen right on the edge, inches from the blue.
blocks.explanation_zh: 重击把他打到悬崖边，手指摸空后才意识到差点坠落。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他几乎因自己的谈判招来坠落；语言策略并不神奇，必须承受真实身体成本。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “More to say?” Mord held the strap between his fists and gave it a sharp pull. The snap made Tyrion jump. The turnkey laughed.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord用皮带响声继续恐吓。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: He won’t push me over, Tyrion told himself desperately as he crawled away from the edge. Catelyn Stark wants me alive, he doesn’t dare kill me. He wiped the blood off his lips with the back of his hand, grinned, and said, “That was a stiff one, Mord.” The gaoler squinted at him, trying to decide if he was being mocked. “I could make good use of a strong man like you.” The strap flew at him, but this time Tyrion was able to cringe away from it. He took a glancing blow to the shoulder, nothing more. “Gold,” he repeated, scrambling backward like a crab, “more gold than you’ll see here in a lifetime. Enough to buy land, women, horses . . . you could be a lord. Lord Mord.” Tyrion hawked up a glob of blood and phlegm and spat it out into the sky.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion赌Catelyn要他活着，以Lord Mord和土地女人马匹继续描画报酬。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Lord Mord是有意夸张的身份诱饵：Tyrion把自己熟悉的等级欲望翻译给看守。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “Is no gold,” Mord said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord质疑这里根本没有gold。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: He’s listening! Tyrion thought. “They relieved me of my purse when they captured me, but the gold is still mine. Catelyn Stark might take a man prisoner, but she’d never stoop to rob him. That wouldn’t be honorable. Help me, and all the gold is yours.” Mord’s strap licked out, but it was a halfhearted, desultory swing, slow and contemptuous. Tyrion caught the leather in his hand and held it prisoned. “There will be no risk to you. All you need do is deliver a message.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion察觉他已在听，解释钱袋仍属自己，只需Mord送一条message。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mord半心半意挥带说明gold已改变暴力节奏；Tyrion用这个细节判断谈判窗口打开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: The gaoler yanked his leather strap free of Tyrion’s grasp. “Message,” he said, as if he had never heard the word before. His frown made deep creases in his brow.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord第一次认真咀嚼message这个概念。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “You heard me, my lord. Only carry my word to your lady. Tell her . . . ” What? What would possibly make Lysa Anyn relent? The inspiration came to Tyrion Lannister suddenly. “ . . . .tell her that I wish to confess my crimes.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion灵机一动，要Mord告诉Lysa自己愿意confess。
blocks.literary_note_zh: confess是完美message，因为它同时满足Lysa的确信、公开欲和道德优越感。
blocks.background_zh: **confession：** Tyrion承诺的是‘承认我的罪’，没有明确承诺承认Lysa指定的两项murder charge。

blocks.original_text: Mord raised his arm and Tyrion braced himself for another blow, but the turnkey hesitated. Suspicion and greed warred in his eyes. He wanted that gold, yet he feared a trick; he had the look of a man who had often been tricked. “Is lie,” he muttered darkly. “Dwarf man cheat me.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord在greed与疑心间犹豫，怕被欺骗。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “I will put my promise in writing,” Tyrion vowed.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion承诺把gold promise写下来。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Some illiterates held writing in disdain; others seemed to have a superstitious reverence for the written word, as if it were some sort of magic. Fortunately, Mord was one of the latter. The turnkey lowered the strap. “Writing down gold. Much gold.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord把writing视作近乎magic，终于降低皮带。
blocks.literary_note_zh: writing对Mord具有制度与魔法双重权威；Tyrion利用文化差异让口头承诺变得可信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, much gold,” Tyrion assured him. “The purse is just a taste, my friend. My brother wears armor of solid gold plate.” In truth, Jaime’s armor was gilded steel, but this oaf would never know the difference.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion夸大Jaime盔甲为纯金，进一步强化财富想象。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Mord fingered his strap thoughtfully, but in the end, he relented and went to fetch paper and ink. When the letter was written, the gaoler frowned at it suspiciously. “Now deliver my message,” Tyrion urged.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord最终取来纸墨并送信。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他承受Mord殴打，以gold、writing和confession逐步让看守送信。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: He was shivering in his sleep when they came for him, late that night. Mord opened the door but kept his silence. Ser Vardis Egen woke Tyrion with the point of his boot. “On your feet, Imp. My lady wants to see you.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 深夜Ser Vardis来sky cell唤醒Tyrion见Lysa。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion被带回High Hall，取回cloak并确认Vale nobles、Marillion和Bronn都在场，为公开表演搭好舞台。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion rubbed the sleep from his eyes and put on a grimace he scarcely felt. “No doubt she does, but what makes you think I wish to see her?”

blocks.literary_note_zh: 故作不愿见Lysa是在guards面前掩盖主动求见，防止对方意识到自己正按Tyrion计划行动。

blocks.original_text: Ser Vardis frowned. Tyrion remembered him well from the years he had spent at King’s Landing as the captain of the Hand’s household guard. A square, plain face, silver hair, a heavy build, and no humor whatsoever. “Your wishes are not my concern. On your feet, or I’ll have you carried.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Vardis不理会他的意愿，命他自行起身否则被抬走。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion被带回High Hall，取回cloak并确认Vale nobles、Marillion和Bronn都在场，为公开表演搭好舞台。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion clambered awkwardly to his feet. “A cold night,” he said casually, “and the High Hall is so drafty. I don’t wish to catch a chill. Mord, if you would be so good, fetch my cloak.”

blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion被带回High Hall，取回cloak并确认Vale nobles、Marillion和Bronn都在场，为公开表演搭好舞台。

blocks.explanation_zh: Mord怀疑地看着他。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion被带回High Hall，取回cloak并确认Vale nobles、Marillion和Bronn都在场，为公开表演搭好舞台。

blocks.original_text: “My cloak,” Tyrion repeated. “The shadowskin you took from me for safekeeping. You recall.”

blocks.literary_note_zh: for safekeeping用礼貌词包装盗取，让Ser Vardis能在不审理争议的情况下顺手纠正它。

blocks.original_text: Mord did not dare grumble. He gave Tyrion a glare that promised future retribution, yet he went for the cloak. When he draped it around his prisoner’s neck, Tyrion smiled. “My thanks. I shall think of you whenever I wear it.” He flung the trailing end of the long fur over his right shoulder, and felt warm for the first time in days. “Lead on, Ser Vardis.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mord只得服从；Tyrion取回财物与温暖，并以含蓄话语警告自己会记住他。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 取回cloak是小型胜利：他借更高层命令迫使Mord归还物品，也重新建立体面外观。

blocks.original_text: The High Hall of the Arryns was aglow with the light of fifty torches, burning in the sconces along the walls. The Lady Lysa wore black silk, with the moon-and-falcon sewn on her breast in pearls. Since she did not look the sort to join the Night’s Watch, Tyrion could only imagine that she had decided mourning clothes were appropriate garb for a confession. Her long auburn hair, woven into an elaborate braid, fell across her left shoulder. The taller throne beside her was empty; no doubt the little Lord of the Eyrie was off shaking in his sleep. Tyrion was thankful for that much, at least.
blocks.explanation_zh: High Hall灯火通明，Lysa穿mourning black准备听confession，Robert不在场。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion被带回High Hall，取回cloak并确认Vale nobles、Marillion和Bronn都在场，为公开表演搭好舞台。

blocks.original_text: He bowed deeply and took a moment to glance around the hall. Lady Arryn had summoned her knights and retainers to hear his confession, as he had hoped. He saw Ser Brynden Tully’s craggy face and Lord Nestor Royce’s bluff one. Beside Nestor stood a younger man with fierce black side-whiskers who could only be his heir, Ser Albar. Most of the principal houses of the Vale were represented. Tyrion noted Ser Lyn Corbray, slender as a sword, Lord Hunter with his gouty legs, the widowed Lady Waynwood surrounded by her sons. Others sported sigils he did not know; broken lance, green viper, burning tower, winged chalice.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion确认Vale主要家族被召集为观众，正中计划。
blocks.literary_note_zh: confession的真正对象不只是Lysa，而是整个Vale公共圈；程序能否被扭曲取决于是否有人见证。
blocks.background_zh: **Vale houses：** Royce、Corbray、Hunter、Waynwood等主要贵族在场，使这次会面具有公开政治见证性质。

blocks.original_text: Among the lords of the Vale were several of his companions from the high road; Ser Rodrik Cassel, pale from half-healed wounds, stood with Ser Willis Wode beside him. Marillion the singer had found a new woodharp. Tyrion smiled; whatever happened here tonight, he did not wish it to happen in secret, and there was no one like a singer for spreading a story near and far.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn一行也在；Marillion有新harp，正适合把事件传播出去。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Marillion把现场变成未来舆论场；Tyrion不需控制每个knight，只需让故事能离开Eyrie。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: In the rear of the hall, Bronn lounged beneath a pillar. The freerider’s black eyes were fixed on Tyrion, and his hand lay lightly on the pommel of his sword. Tyrion gave him a long look, wondering . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn在后方靠柱观望，手放剑柄；Tyrion开始计算可能性。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 对Bronn的长注视是无声试探；文本暂不说明两人是否已有明确约定。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn Stark spoke first. “You wish to confess your crimes, we are told.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn先问他是否要confess。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion被带回High Hall，取回cloak并确认Vale nobles、Marillion和Bronn都在场，为公开表演搭好舞台。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “I do, my lady,” Tyrion answered.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion确认。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion被带回High Hall，取回cloak并确认Vale nobles、Marillion和Bronn都在场，为公开表演搭好舞台。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Lysa Arryn smiled at her sister. “The sky cells always break them. The gods can see them there, and there is no darkness to hide in.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa炫耀sky cells总能击溃囚犯。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Lysa把sky cell效果归于gods可见，借宗教语言遮蔽饥饿、寒冷、斜坡与殴打这些人造压力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “He does not look broken to me,” Lady Catelyn said.

blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion被带回High Hall，取回cloak并确认Vale nobles、Marillion和Bronn都在场，为公开表演搭好舞台。

blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa命Tyrion开口。

blocks.original_text: And now to roll the dice, he thought with another quick glance back at Bronn. “Where to begin? I am a vile little man, I confess it. My crimes and sins are beyond counting, my lords and ladies. I have lain with whores, not once but hundreds of times. I have wished my own lord father dead, and my sister, our gracious queen, as well.” Behind him, someone chuckled. “I have not always treated my servants with kindness. I have gambled. I have even cheated, I blush to admit. I have said many cruel and malicious things about the noble lords and ladies of the court.” That drew outright laughter. “Once I—”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion以真实但无关的个人恶习作夸张自白，把审判变成喜剧并引发笑声。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion的内容大多真实，却不响应指控；他利用confession一词的宽泛含义揭露Lysa只想要承认而非调查。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “Silence!” Lysa Arryn’s pale round face had turned a burning pink. “What do you imagine you are doing, dwarf?”

blocks.literary_note_zh: 假confession激怒Lysa后，Tyrion诉诸公开justice取得trial，再以trial by combat避开Robert控制的裁决。

blocks.literary_note_zh: 假confession激怒Lysa后，Tyrion诉诸公开justice取得trial，再以trial by combat避开Robert控制的裁决。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn Stark took a step forward. “You are accused of sending a hired knife to slay my son Bran in his bed, and of conspiring to murder Lord Jon Arryn, the Hand of the King.”

blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn把表演重新锚定具体charges，这也第一次让公开观众听清案件范围。

blocks.original_text: Lady Lysa rose from her weirwood throne. “I will not be made mock of. You have had your little jape, Imp. I trust you enjoyed it. Ser Vardis, take him back to the dungeon . . . but this time find him a smaller cell, with a floor more sharply sloped.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa认为受辱，命把他送回更危险的cell。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 假confession激怒Lysa后，Tyrion诉诸公开justice取得trial，再以trial by combat避开Robert控制的裁决。

blocks.original_text: “Is this how justice is done in the Vale?” Tyrion roared, so loudly that Ser Vardis froze for an instant. “Does honor stop at the Bloody Gate? You accuse me of crimes, I deny them, so you throw me into an open cell to freeze and starve.” He lifted his head, to give them all a good look at the bruises Mord had left on his face. “Where is the king’s justice? Is the Eyrie not part of the Seven Kingdoms? I stand accused, you say. Very well. I demand a trial! Let me speak, and let my truth or falsehood be judged openly, in the sight of gods and men.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion突然高声诉诸Vale honor、king’s justice和公开trial，并展示脸上伤痕。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion把私人受虐转译成king’s justice问题，并展示bruises作为视觉证据，迫使旁观贵族考虑自身制度名誉。
blocks.background_zh: **king’s justice：** Vale属于Seven Kingdoms，贵族囚犯可要求正式审理；Tyrion用王国共同规范挑战Lysa私人处置。

blocks.original_text: A low murmuring filled the High Hall. He had her, Tyrion knew. He was highborn, the son of the most powerful lord in the realm, the brother of the queen. He could not be denied a trial. Guardsmen in sky-blue cloaks had started toward Tyrion, but Ser Vardis bid them halt and looked to Lady Lysa.
blocks.explanation_zh: 厅内议论；Tyrion知道其高贵身份使Lysa难以拒绝trial。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 假confession激怒Lysa后，Tyrion诉诸公开justice取得trial，再以trial by combat避开Robert控制的裁决。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Her small mouth twitched in a petulant smile. “If you are tried and found to be guilty of the crimes for which you stand accused, then by the king’s own laws, you must pay with your life’s blood. We keep no headsman in the Eyrie, my lord of Lannister. Open the Moon Door.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa接受审判，却以Moon Door作为死刑威胁。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Lysa表面接受trial，立即以处刑装置预定结论；程序形式与裁决控制仍在同一人手中。
blocks.background_zh: **Moon Door：** High Hall外墙上的门，门外直接是高空；Eyrie以此执行坠落死刑。

blocks.original_text: The press of spectators parted. A narrow weirwood door stood between two slender marble pillars, a crescent moon carved in the white wood. Those standing closest edged backward as a pair of guardsmen marched through. One man removed the heavy bronze bars; the second pulled the door inward. Their blue cloaks rose snapping from their shoulders, caught in the sudden gust of wind that came howling through the open door. Beyond was the emptiness of the night sky, speckled with cold uncaring stars.
blocks.explanation_zh: guards打开Moon Door，夜空与狂风进入hall。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Moon Door把‘open court’字面化为致命开口；冷星与风让自然景观成为权力剧场。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “Behold the king’s justice,” Lysa Arryn said. Torch flames fluttered like pennons along the walls, and here and there the odd torch guttered out.
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa称这就是king’s justice，火炬在风中摇灭。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 假confession激怒Lysa后，Tyrion诉诸公开justice取得trial，再以trial by combat避开Robert控制的裁决。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “Lysa, I think this unwise,” Catelyn Stark said as the black wind swirled around the hall.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn认为此举不明智。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 假confession激怒Lysa后，Tyrion诉诸公开justice取得trial，再以trial by combat避开Robert控制的裁决。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Her sister ignored her. “You want a trial, my lord of Lannister. Very well, a trial you shall have. My son will listen to whatever you care to say, and you shall hear his judgment. Then you may leave . . . by one door or the other.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa仍提出由Robert听审，再决定Tyrion从哪扇门离开。
blocks.literary_note_zh: one door or the other是精心包装的假选择：正常离开与坠落被描述成同一审判的两个出口。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: She looked so pleased with herself, Tyrion thought, and small wonder. How could a trial threaten her, when her weakling son was the lord judge? Tyrion glanced at her Moon Door. Mother, I want to see him fly! the boy had said. How many men had the snot-nosed little wretch sent through that door already?
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion看穿Robert作judge必受Lysa控制，也想起孩子想看他fly。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 假confession激怒Lysa后，Tyrion诉诸公开justice取得trial，再以trial by combat避开Robert控制的裁决。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: “I thank you, my good lady, but I see no need to trouble Lord Robert,” Tyrion said politely. “The gods know the truth of my innocence. I will have their verdict, not the judgment of men. I demand trial by combat.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 他绕过人审，依法要求trial by combat让gods裁决。
blocks.literary_note_zh: trial by combat把裁决者从Robert移到gods/武力，并调用Lysa不能公开否认的贵族法律传统。
blocks.background_zh: **trial by combat：** 被告要求由单斗让gods显示正当一方的审判方式；贵族法律承认此项权利。

blocks.original_text: A storm of sudden laughter filled the High Hall of the Arryns. Lord Nestor Royce snorted, Ser Willis chuckled, Ser Lyn Corbray guffawed, and others threw back their heads and howled until tears ran down their faces. Marillion clumsily plucked a gay note on his new woodharp with the fingers of his broken hand. Even the wind seemed to whistle with derision as it came skirling through the Moon Door.
blocks.explanation_zh: 众人因Tyrion身体条件而哄笑，连风声都像嘲弄。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 笑声准确指出Tyrion若亲战的身体劣势，却也使众人低估他可要求champion的下一步。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: Lysa Arryn’s watery blue eyes looked uncertain. He had caught her off balance. “You have that right, to be sure.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa措手不及，却承认他确有此权。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Lysa的uncertain表明她熟悉权利存在，却没预见Tyrion会把它用于改变审判结构。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在sky cell的心理压力、Tyrion的语言策略、Vale公开司法与trial by combat。

blocks.original_text: The young knight with the green viper embroidered on his surcoat stepped forward and went to one knee. “My lady, I beg the boon of championing your cause.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 一名green viper knight抢先请求为Lysa出战。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Vale knights争当Lysa champion；Tyrion借champion对称权和ballad声誉逼她让步，最终由Bronn应战。
blocks.background_zh: **champion：** 当事人可由代理战士出战。Lysa先选择己方champion，也给Tyrion主张同等选择留下程序空间。

blocks.original_text: “The honor should be mine,” old Lord Hunter said. “For the love I bore your lord husband, let me avenge his death.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lord Hunter也以爱Jon Arryn为由请战。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Vale knights争当Lysa champion；Tyrion借champion对称权和ballad声誉逼她让步，最终由Bronn应战。

blocks.original_text: “My father served Lord Jon faithfully as High Steward of the Vale,” Ser Albar Royce boomed. “Let me serve his son in this.”

blocks.literary_note_zh: Vale knights争当Lysa champion；Tyrion借champion对称权和ballad声誉逼她让步，最终由Bronn应战。

blocks.original_text: “The gods favor the man with the just cause,” said Ser Lyn Corbray, “yet often that turns out to be the man with the surest sword. We all know who that is.” He smiled modestly.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Lyn Corbray把正义与最强剑手巧妙并置，自荐为champion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Ser Lyn的话把神意与剑术悄然等同，揭示trial by combat如何把‘truth’交给战斗能力。

blocks.original_text: A dozen other men all spoke at once, clamoring to be heard. Tyrion found it disheartening to realize so many strangers were eager to kill him. Perhaps this had not been such a clever plan after all.
blocks.explanation_zh: 多人争相杀他，使Tyrion一度怀疑计划是否聪明。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion并非全知布局者；当champions争抢时，他也真实感到策略可能失控。

blocks.original_text: Lady Lysa raised a hand for silence. “I thank you, my lords, as I know my son would thank you if he were among us. No men in the Seven Kingdoms are as bold and true as the knights of the Vale. Would that I could grant you all this honor. Yet I can choose only one.” She gestured. “Ser Vardis Egen, you were ever my lord husband’s good right hand. You shall be our champion.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa最终指定沉默的Ser Vardis为己方champion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Vale knights争当Lysa champion；Tyrion借champion对称权和ballad声誉逼她让步，最终由Bronn应战。
blocks.background_zh: **Ser Vardis Egen：** Jon Arryn生前household guard captain，现为Lysa服务；经验丰富，但对明显不对等决斗感到羞耻。

blocks.original_text: Ser Vardis had been singularly silent. “My lady,” he said gravely, sinking to one knee, “pray give this burden to another, I have no taste for it. The man is no warrior. Look at him. A dwarf, half my size and lame in the legs. It would be shameful to slaughter such a man and call it justice.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Vardis认为屠杀矮小跛足的Tyrion有辱justice，请她另选。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Ser Vardis的拒绝不是支持Tyrion无罪，而是担心明显不对等的屠杀玷污knightly honor与justice之名。

blocks.original_text: Oh, excellent, Tyrion thought. “I agree.”

blocks.original_text: Lysa glared at him. “You demanded a trial by combat.”

blocks.literary_note_zh: Vale knights争当Lysa champion；Tyrion借champion对称权和ballad声誉逼她让步，最终由Bronn应战。

blocks.original_text: “And now I demand a champion, such as you have chosen for yourself. My brother Jaime will gladly take my part, I know.”

blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion借Lysa已选champion这一先例主张对称权利；Jaime既是理想战力，也是拖延时间的工具。

blocks.original_text: “Your precious Kingslayer is hundreds of leagues from here,” snapped Lysa Arryn.

blocks.original_text: “Singer,” Tyrion said, turning to Marillion, “when you make a ballad of this, be certain you tell them how Lady Arryn denied the dwarf the right to a champion, and sent him forth lame and bruised and hobbling to face her finest knight.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion转向Marillion，预演一首传播Lysa剥夺残疾囚犯champion权的ballad。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他不直接争法条，而是把未来ballad写进当前现场，以声誉成本逼Lysa兑现公平外观。

blocks.original_text: “I deny you nothing!” Lysa Arryn said, her voice peeved and shrill with irritation. “Name your champion, Imp . . . if you think you can find a man to die for you.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Lysa受公共名誉压力，怒允他自行寻找champion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Lysa说I deny you nothing，证明公开羞辱叙事已迫使她改口；观众和singer共同构成约束。

blocks.original_text: “If it is all the same to you, I’d sooner find one to kill for me.” Tyrion looked over the long hall. No one moved. For a long moment he wondered if it had all been a colossal blunder.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion说要找替自己杀人的人；全场沉默令他以为豪赌失败。
blocks.literary_note_zh: kill for me把浪漫的die for you改成雇佣逻辑，准确预示Tyrion寻找的不是忠臣而是利益一致者。

blocks.original_text: Then there was a stirring in the rear of the chamber. “I’ll stand for the dwarf,” Bronn called out.

blocks.literary_note_zh: Bronn从rear发声回应此前Tyrion的目光；他选择的是高风险机会，而非公开宣誓私人爱戴。
